what_it_does: Stores metadata for a channel attachment that points to a board item.
observations: The file contains only YAML frontmatter and no body content.
standards: The target URI is the only substantive payload beyond identity/timestamp fields.
It is a bare ledger entry with no explanatory text.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added fields.
Represents a cross-reference using a 'board://' URI scheme.
Stores channel memory attachments as individual markdown files named by attachment id.
concerns: No body or context explains why this attachment exists.
The board item target cannot be verified from this file alone.
No author or source message is recorded.
notes: A minimal channel-attachment ledger entry that links attachment att-eb4d128f to board item item-83bd8f5d with a timestamp.
questions: What content or discussion does board item item-83bd8f5d represent?
What does attachment_type 'board_items' imply about expected behavior?
Is this attachment generated automatically or manually?
purpose_vs_actual: The implied purpose is to record an attachment-to-board-item reference, and the file does exactly that with identity, type, target, and timestamp metadata.
intention_signals: It appears intended to create a durable, timestamped association between a channel attachment and a specific board item for later retrieval or graph traversal.
contribution: Provides a concrete, timestamped attachment-to-board-item reference that a fused system can use to build a traceability edge between channel memory and board items.
summary_for_embedding: Attachment metadata record att-eb4d128f for the dragnet-development channel, typed as board_items, targeting board item item-83bd8f5d and recording an added timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: To record that this channel attachment was attached to board item item-83bd8f5d at the given time.
